name: BasicTheories.ListTheory

values.name: lengthOfNonEmptyList
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List a) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList -> RefinementProofs.Proven Basics.Int BasicTheories.NumberTheory.Positive

values.name: nonEmptyListMap
values.type: (a -> b) -> RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List a) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList -> RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List b) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList

name: BasicTheories.NumberTheory

    , proveNonEmptyString

values.name: lengthOfNonEmptyString
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven String.String BasicTheories.StringTheory.NonEmptyString -> RefinementProofs.Proven Basics.Int BasicTheories.NumberTheory.Positive

name: RefinementProofs

unions.name: Implies
unions.comment: Implies that if "a" holds than "b" holds

unions.name: Proven
unions.comment: A value "a" that is proven to hold the property "b"

values.name: absurd
values.comment: Used by library writers to carefully ignore "impossible" states

values.name: and
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a And proof

values.name: axiom
values.comment: Used by library writers to create axioms with non-exported constructors

values.name: elimAndL
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a b

values.name: elimAndR
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a c

values.name: elimOrL
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a c

values.name: elimOrR
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a b

values.name: exorcise
values.comment: Extract the actual value and remove the proof

values.name: imply
values.comment: Used by library writers to imply proof relations

values.name: introOrL
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or p2 p1)

values.name: introOrR
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or p1 p2)

values.name: inverse
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a p1)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1))

values.name: makeAnd
values.comment: A convience method to check two proofs at once
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a b)) -> (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a c)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c))

values.name: makeOr
values.comment: A convience method to check two proofs
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a b)) -> (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a c)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or b c))

values.name: modusPonens
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p2

values.name: modusTollens
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p2)

values.name: not
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a Not proof

values.name: or
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a Or proof

values.name: provenOrInversed
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a p1)) -> a -> Result.Result (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1)) (RefinementProofs.Proven a p1)

values.name: since
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p2

values.name: sinceNot
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p2)
